name: AI-enhanced Educational Game Design
description: Awarded to learners who have demonstrated, with evidence, that they can take a learning problem from a defensible design statement through a target-learner-tested prototype to an implementation-ready specification. Non-compensatory: the credential requires Proficient performance on 25 rubric criteria across five deliverables.

criteria.narrative: The learner must reach **Proficient** on every criterion of every deliverable (D1–D5). There are 25 criteria total, and they are non-compensatory — a high score on one deliverable does not substitute for a low score on another. Evidence is the artifact itself plus a dated change log for each deliverable. An Exemplary rating is recognized but not required.

alignment.targetDescription: Educators design authentic, learner-driven activities and environments that recognize and accommodate learner variability.
Educators facilitate learning with technology to support student achievement of standards.
Educators understand and use data to drive their instruction and support students in achieving their learning goals.
Designs learning environments that provide multiple means of representation, action and expression, and engagement.
Apply design practices that close the Digital Design Divide by centering learner voice, context, and accessibility.
Develop and produce educational materials, curricula, and programmes.
Employ various approaches, learning styles and channels to instruct students.
Use ICT tools and resources to create knowledge and innovate processes and products.

alignment.targetName: Lightcast Open Skills — Learning Experience Design
alignment.targetDescription: Designing learning experiences grounded in learner analysis, measurable objectives, and iteration.
Designing formative and summative assessments aligned to stated learning outcomes.
Producing low-fidelity prototypes for rapid user testing and iteration.

_upgrade_note: OBv3 is the W3C Verifiable Credentials 2.0 expression of Open Badges. Consumers that only speak OBv2 should read /credential/badge-class.json; wallet-ready clients (e.g. DCC Learner Credential Wallet) should read this v3 file. See /docs/L1-obv3-upgrade.md.
